message:
refactor(db,tui): replace GROUP_CONCAT JOINs with batched ID lookups and harden SQLite resilience

Eliminate expensive GROUP BY / GROUP_CONCAT / multi-JOIN patterns in the
explorer, messages, threads, and projects TUI screens.  Each screen now
fetches raw message rows first, then resolves sender names, project slugs,
and recipient lists via batched IN-clause queries (agent_names_by_id,
project_slugs_by_id, recipient_names_by_message).  The threads screen
reads recipients_json directly instead of joining message_recipients.

CLI preflight banner switches from MAX(id) / sqlite_sequence heuristics
to accurate COUNT(*) queries so sparse-ID tables report correct totals.
Schema bootstrap detection now checks for missing columns (not just
tables) against a canonical column list.

DB layer improvements:
- Generalize open-with-lock-retry into retry_sqlite_lock_impl and add
  execute_sql_with_lock_retry so PRAGMA and init SQL also retry on
  transient SQLITE_BUSY/locked errors.
- detect_timestamp_format distinguishes non-empty legacy schemas from
  truly empty databases, avoiding misclassifying populated tables as
  TimestampFormat::Empty.
- search_v3 backfill gracefully handles missing messages table, clears
  stale index data, and adds sqlite_error_is_missing_table helper.

TUI poller adds refill_missing_detail_lists_from_sqlite fallback that
queries SQLite directly when the snapshot's detail lists are empty but
counts are non-zero.  Projects screen can recover its list from SQLite
when the poller snapshot is incomplete.

Dashboard avoids unnecessary ChartTransition::set_target calls and only
requests fast ticks when the trend panel is visible and actually
animating (new ChartTransition::is_animating method).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
